perturbation.original_text: Information under this Agreement is defined as any data or knowledge from Bomatec or the Business Partner that Bomatec has made or will make available to the Business Partner, or vice versa, in verbal, written or electronic form or in the form of one or several objects, irrespective of whether such data and knowledge was designated as confidential or secret.
perturbation.changed_text: Information under this Agreement is defined as any data or knowledge from Bomatec or the Business Partner that Bomatec has made or will make available to the Business Partner, or vice versa, in verbal, written or electronic form or in the form of one or several objects, only if such data and knowledge was explicitly designated as confidential or secret at the time of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition includes all data/knowledge exchanged, regardless of explicit designation. The modified version limits it to information explicitly labeled confidential/secret upon disclosure. This in-text contradiction causes conflict, because under the original definition all information shared would be considered confidential, however, with the change, if not explicitly declared confidential at time of disclosure, the information would not be regarded as such.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The contractual partners undertake to treat confidentially any information they obtain, to use it only for the intended purpose of the particular assignment or project and not to make it di rectly or indirectly accessible to third parties. Furthermore, they undertake to place a duty of confidentiality on their employees and any sub -contractors that correspond to the one contained in this Agreement. The duty of confidentiality does not apply to information - that the receiving contractual partner was already in possession of when the information was passed on, - that was published when or after it was disclosed without being in breach of this Non - Disclosure Agreement, - that a contractual partner received legally from a third party where there was no duty of confidentiality, - that a contractual partner develops independently of the confidential information after this Agreement has come into force, - that a contractual partner has released as a result of a written agreement with the other contractual partner.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Information' broadly, encompassing all data/knowledge exchanged regardless of whether it was explicitly designated as confidential. The changed text narrows this definition to include only data/knowledge explicitly designated as confidential/secret at the time of disclosure. This change directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text,' which imposes a duty of confidentiality on 'any information' obtained. Under the original definition, 'any information' would be considered confidential, triggering the duty. However, with the changed definition, only explicitly designated information is confidential, potentially excluding information that would have been protected under the original definition and creating a direct conflict regarding the scope of confidentiality obligations. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', as different sections now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners undertake to destroy immediately any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, unless such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners undertake to destroy immediately any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, unless such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership. However, all financial records must be retained for a minimum of seven years, regardless of project status.
perturbation.explanation: The original mandates immediate destruction unless needed for follow-up. The modified version adds a contradictory requirement to retain financial records for seven years, irrespective of project status, creating in-text contradiction regarding the document retention policy after project completion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Destruction of documents and saved information Upon completion of an assignment or project, the contractual partners undertake to destroy immediately any information or objects given to them that they or any sub -contractors may have, unless such information and property are required for any outstanding or future follow -up activities under the mutual business partnership. However, all financial records must be retained for a minimum of seven years, regardless of project status. The destruction of i nformation in electronic form also applies to e- mails, data storage media such as USB sticks, local hard disks, servers and back -up copies.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates immediate destruction of information upon completion of a project, unless needed for follow-up activities. The changed text adds a clause that financial records must be retained for seven years, regardless of project status. This directly contradicts the original instruction for immediate destruction, creating an inconsistency regarding document retention policy after project completion. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Changes or additions to this Agreement (including the waiver of this provision) must be made in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Changes or additions to this Agreement (including the waiver of this provision) must be made in writing. However, minor amendments may be agreed verbally between project managers of both parties and shall be binding if documented in meeting minutes.
perturbation.explanation: The original insists on written changes. The modified text introduces an exception for minor amendments agreed verbally and documented in meeting minutes. This in-text contradiction creates ambiguity and uncertainty, as it is unclear what constitutes a 'minor amendment' and whether those verbal agreements would be legally binding.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Written form Changes or additions to this Agreement (including the waiver of this provision) must be made in writing. However, minor amendments may be agreed verbally between project managers of both parties and shall be binding if documented in meeting minutes.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all changes must be in writing. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing verbal agreements for minor amendments under certain conditions. The contradicted text and changed text are located in the same place. Thus, the change introduces a clear contradiction to the original requirement of written changes, creating an inconsistency and potential for dispute.
